title: Regional Airline Margin Decline
background: A regional airline has seen profit margins decline over the past two years despite stable passenger volume. The CEO wants to understand what is driving the decline and what actions the company should take.
candidate_prompt: What factors would you investigate to determine why profitability is falling, and what recommendations would you make?
good_answer_includes: Breaks profit into revenue and cost drivers
Looks at ticket pricing, passenger volume, and route mix
Considers fixed and variable costs
Identifies external factors such as fuel prices and competition
great_answer_includes: Segments profitability by route
Analyzes load factor and pricing tradeoffs
Considers fuel cost sensitivity
Recommends specific actions such as route rationalization or pricing changes
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should structure the case around revenue, costs, route-level economics, and competitive dynamics.

title: Boutique Fitness Expansion
background: A boutique fitness chain with 30 locations is considering entering Chicago after several years of growth in smaller Midwest markets.
candidate_prompt: Should the client enter the Chicago market, and what factors should they evaluate before making the decision?
good_answer_includes: Evaluates market size and growth
Assesses competition
Considers customer segments
Reviews location-level economics
great_answer_includes: Calculates break-even membership levels
Compares premium versus budget positioning
Suggests a pilot launch before full expansion
Identifies real estate and customer acquisition risks
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should consider market attractiveness, financial viability, competitive dynamics, and implementation risk.

title: Urban Grocery Store Margin Decline
background: A regional grocery chain operates 85 stores across several large metro areas. Over the past 18 months, revenue has grown slightly, but operating profit has fallen by 12%. Leadership wants to understand why margins are shrinking and what actions they should take.
candidate_prompt: How would you determine what is causing the profit decline, and what recommendations would you make to improve profitability?
good_answer_includes: Breaks profitability into revenue and cost drivers
Examines changes in basket size, traffic, and pricing
Analyzes labor, rent, shrink, and supplier cost trends
Considers differences across store locations or formats
great_answer_includes: Segments performance by urban, suburban, and premium locations
Identifies product mix shifts toward lower-margin categories
Quantifies impact of shrink and labor cost increases
Recommends targeted pricing, assortment, and operational changes
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should avoid assuming revenue growth means healthy performance and should investigate margin, mix, and cost structure carefully.

title: Business Hotel Profitability Pressure
background: A hotel group owns 40 business-focused hotels near airports and downtown office districts. Occupancy has returned to pre-pandemic levels, but profits remain below historical averages. The CEO wants to know why profitability has not recovered.
candidate_prompt: What factors would you analyze to explain the profit gap, and what actions should the hotel group consider?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue per available room and occupancy
Compares business, leisure, and group travel segments
Reviews labor, utilities, maintenance, and food service costs
Considers changes in customer behavior and pricing power
great_answer_includes: Identifies mix shift from higher-margin corporate travel to lower-rate leisure travel
Quantifies cost increases per occupied room
Evaluates whether amenities should be reduced or repriced
Recommends segment-specific pricing and staffing adjustments
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should separate occupancy recovery from profit recovery and consider rate, mix, and operating cost changes.

title: Premium Gym Chain Profit Drop
background: A premium gym chain operates 60 locations in major U.S. cities. Membership has grown by 8% this year, but total profit has declined. Management is confused because the company appears to be attracting more customers than ever.
candidate_prompt: How would you investigate why profit is falling despite membership growth?
good_answer_includes: Compares membership growth with average revenue per member
Analyzes fixed and variable costs by location
Reviews discounting, churn, and promotional offers
Considers capacity constraints and utilization
great_answer_includes: Identifies whether new members are joining at lower promotional rates
Calculates contribution margin by member type
Evaluates overcrowding effects on retention and premium positioning
Recommends pricing, member segmentation, and location-level cost actions
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should recognize that customer growth can be unprofitable if discounts, churn, or variable costs increase faster than revenue.

title: Low-Cost Airline Route Losses
background: A low-cost airline has added 20 new domestic routes over the past year. While total passengers have increased, the company’s profit margin has dropped from 9% to 4%. Executives want to determine whether the new routes are hurting overall profitability.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the profitability of the new routes and decide what the airline should do next?
good_answer_includes: Compares revenue and cost by route
Analyzes load factor, ticket price, and ancillary revenue
Reviews fuel, crew, airport, and maintenance costs
Considers competitive dynamics on new routes
great_answer_includes: Calculates route-level contribution margin
Distinguishes startup losses from structurally unprofitable routes
Evaluates aircraft utilization and schedule efficiency
Recommends route closures, frequency changes, or pricing adjustments
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should move from company-level averages to route-level economics and distinguish profitable growth from volume growth.

title: Coffee Chain Breakfast Margin Decline
background: A national coffee chain recently expanded its breakfast menu to include hot sandwiches and baked goods. Morning traffic has increased, but store-level margins have declined. The leadership team wants to understand whether the new food strategy is working.
candidate_prompt: How would you analyze the profitability impact of the expanded breakfast menu?
good_answer_includes: Compares incremental revenue from food with incremental costs
Analyzes food margins versus beverage margins
Reviews labor, preparation time, and waste
Considers whether food purchases increase beverage attachment
great_answer_includes: Calculates contribution margin by menu category
Measures cannibalization of higher-margin beverage-only orders
Identifies operational bottlenecks during peak hours
Recommends menu simplification, bundling, or selective store rollout
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should evaluate both direct food profitability and the indirect impact on throughput, attachment, and customer behavior.

title: Online Furniture Retailer Profit Squeeze
background: An online furniture retailer has grown revenue by 20% year over year, but net profit has declined. The company sells sofas, tables, beds, and home office furniture directly to consumers. Leadership wants to understand what is eroding profitability.
candidate_prompt: What would you investigate to identify the causes of declining profit, and what improvements would you recommend?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue growth by product category
Reviews gross margin, shipping, and return costs
Considers customer acquisition costs
Examines discounting and promotional activity
great_answer_includes: Identifies oversized item shipping and return costs as potential margin drains
Calculates profitability by customer acquisition channel
Evaluates whether growth is concentrated in lower-margin categories
Recommends pricing, logistics, and return policy changes
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should recognize that e-commerce growth can hide fulfillment, returns, and acquisition cost problems.

title: Community Hospital Operating Losses
background: A community hospital has experienced three consecutive years of operating losses. Patient volume is stable, but leadership says reimbursement rates are under pressure and staffing costs are rising. The board wants a path back to profitability.
candidate_prompt: How would you diagnose the hospital’s profitability problem and identify potential solutions?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue by payer type and service line
Reviews labor, supplies, and overhead costs
Considers utilization and capacity by department
Examines reimbursement and patient mix trends
great_answer_includes: Segments profitability by service line and payer mix
Identifies high-cost staffing models or agency labor dependence
Evaluates opportunities to shift care to outpatient settings
Recommends a balanced plan that protects care quality while improving margins
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should handle healthcare profit levers carefully and consider reimbursement, service mix, staffing, and quality constraints.

title: SaaS Company Growth Without Profit
background: A B2B SaaS company provides workflow software to mid-sized businesses. Annual recurring revenue has grown 30%, but the company remains unprofitable. Investors are pressuring management to improve margins without stopping growth entirely.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate why the company is unprofitable and what steps it should take?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue growth, pricing, and customer segments
Reviews customer acquisition cost and sales efficiency
Examines churn and retention
Assesses product development and support costs
great_answer_includes: Calculates lifetime value versus customer acquisition cost
Segments profitability by customer size or sales channel
Identifies whether growth is coming from low-value customers
Recommends pricing, sales efficiency, and retention initiatives
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should focus on unit economics, not just total revenue growth.

title: Theme Park Attendance Recovery, Profit Decline
background: A regional theme park has seen attendance return to historical levels after several weak seasons. However, profits are still 15% below prior peaks. Management wants to understand why the recovery in visitors has not translated into profit.
candidate_prompt: What would you analyze to explain the profit decline and improve performance?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes ticket revenue and attendance trends
Reviews food, merchandise, and parking revenue
Examines labor, maintenance, and utility costs
Considers customer mix and pricing changes
great_answer_includes: Calculates per-guest spending across major categories
Identifies whether attendance growth is driven by discounted tickets
Evaluates labor scheduling and ride maintenance costs
Recommends pricing, bundling, and in-park spending initiatives
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should look beyond attendance and focus on per-guest economics and operating cost inflation.

title: Casual Dining Chain Profitability Decline
background: A casual dining chain with 120 locations has experienced a 10% decline in profit over the past year. Same-store sales are flat, but management believes costs have increased across the business.
candidate_prompt: How would you determine what is driving the decline in profit and what actions the chain should take?
good_answer_includes: Breaks profit into revenue and cost components
Reviews food, labor, rent, and utility costs
Analyzes traffic, average check size, and menu mix
Considers regional or store-level performance differences
great_answer_includes: Identifies menu items with poor margin contribution
Quantifies labor productivity by location
Evaluates pricing opportunities without hurting traffic
Recommends menu engineering and operational improvements
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should use a simple profitability framework but apply it at the store and menu-item level.

title: Last-Mile Delivery Profit Problem
background: A last-mile delivery company serves retailers in 12 metro areas. Delivery volume has increased substantially, but the company is losing money in several markets. Leadership wants to identify why some cities are profitable while others are not.
candidate_prompt: How would you analyze market-level profitability and recommend improvements?
good_answer_includes: Compares revenue and cost by market
Analyzes delivery density, route efficiency, and driver costs
Reviews pricing by customer or contract
Considers order volume variability and service requirements
great_answer_includes: Calculates cost per delivery by market and route type
Identifies density thresholds required for profitability
Segments customers by margin contribution
Recommends market exits, pricing changes, or route optimization
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should recognize the importance of density economics in delivery businesses.

title: Streaming Service Subscriber Growth Losses
background: A niche streaming service focused on documentaries has doubled its subscriber base in two years, but losses have widened. The company wants to understand whether the growth strategy is sustainable.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate why the streaming service is losing more money despite subscriber growth?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes subscription revenue and pricing
Reviews content production and licensing costs
Examines customer acquisition cost and churn
Considers subscriber engagement and retention
great_answer_includes: Calculates payback period on customer acquisition spend
Segments subscribers by tenure and profitability
Evaluates fixed content costs versus subscriber scale
Recommends pricing tiers, content focus, or marketing efficiency improvements
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should distinguish between scalable fixed costs and variable acquisition or retention costs.

title: Athletic Apparel Brand Margin Compression
background: An athletic apparel brand sells through its own website, retail stores, and wholesale partners. Revenue has grown, but gross margins have declined for four consecutive quarters. The CFO wants to identify the root cause.
candidate_prompt: What would you analyze to determine why margins are falling, and what should the company do?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue and margin by channel
Reviews product mix and discounting
Examines manufacturing, freight, and inventory costs
Considers wholesale versus direct-to-consumer economics
great_answer_includes: Identifies channel mix shifts that reduce average margin
Quantifies markdown and inventory clearance impact
Evaluates product-level margin performance
Recommends assortment, pricing, and channel strategy changes
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should examine both channel mix and product mix rather than assuming all revenue growth is equally valuable.

title: Auto Repair Chain Profit Decline
background: A chain of auto repair shops has 75 locations across the Southeast. Customer visits have remained steady, but profit per shop has declined over the past year. Management wants to understand the cause and improve shop performance.
candidate_prompt: How would you investigate the decline in profit per shop?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes average ticket size and service mix
Reviews technician labor costs and productivity
Examines parts costs and supplier pricing
Compares performance across locations
great_answer_includes: Identifies low-margin services taking up technician capacity
Measures revenue per labor hour
Evaluates parts sourcing and inventory practices
Recommends service mix, pricing, and scheduling improvements
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should focus on shop-level productivity and service mix.

title: Retail Pharmacy Profitability Challenge
background: A retail pharmacy chain operates 300 stores. Prescription volume has increased, but overall profits have declined. The CEO wants to understand how prescription economics, front-of-store sales, and operating costs are affecting performance.
candidate_prompt: How would you diagnose the profitability issue and identify potential improvements?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes prescription revenue, reimbursement, and volume
Reviews front-of-store sales and margins
Examines labor, rent, and inventory costs
Considers store-level variation
great_answer_includes: Segments profitability by prescription type and payer
Identifies declining front-of-store contribution as a potential issue
Evaluates staffing model and pharmacist utilization
Recommends store format, service, and assortment changes
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should understand that higher prescription volume may not offset reimbursement pressure or retail margin decline.

title: Construction Contractor Project Losses
background: A commercial construction contractor has won several large projects recently, but overall profit has declined. Some projects are finishing over budget and behind schedule. Management wants to improve project profitability.
candidate_prompt: How would you determine why project profitability is declining and what actions should be taken?
good_answer_includes: Compares estimated versus actual project costs
Analyzes labor, materials, subcontractor, and delay costs
Reviews project bidding and pricing assumptions
Considers project complexity and client changes
great_answer_includes: Identifies whether underbidding is causing unprofitable growth
Quantifies margin impact of delays and change orders
Segments projects by type, size, and profitability
Recommends improved bidding discipline and project controls
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should connect profitability to project selection, pricing, execution, and risk management.

title: Dental Practice Group Margin Decline
background: A dental practice group owns 45 clinics. Patient appointments have increased, but EBITDA margins have declined. Leadership believes the issue may involve staffing, procedure mix, or insurance reimbursement.
candidate_prompt: How would you analyze the margin decline and recommend improvements?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue by procedure type
Reviews payer mix and reimbursement rates
Examines dentist, hygienist, and support staff productivity
Compares profitability across clinics
great_answer_includes: Calculates chair utilization and revenue per provider hour
Identifies mix shift toward lower-margin insured procedures
Evaluates staffing ratios by clinic
Recommends scheduling, payer, and procedure mix improvements
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should consider both clinical capacity utilization and reimbursement economics.

title: Pet Care Chain Profit Slippage
background: A pet care chain offers grooming, boarding, daycare, and retail products. Revenue is up 15%, but profits have grown only 2%. The owner wants to understand why the business is not seeing more operating leverage.
candidate_prompt: How would you investigate why profit growth is lagging behind revenue growth?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue and margin by service line
Reviews labor requirements for each service
Examines pricing, utilization, and capacity
Considers retail product margins
great_answer_includes: Identifies services with high revenue but low labor productivity
Calculates contribution margin by service type
Evaluates capacity constraints in grooming or boarding
Recommends pricing, scheduling, and service mix changes
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should recognize that service businesses depend heavily on labor utilization and mix.

title: Regional Bank Branch Profitability
background: A regional bank operates 180 branches. Digital banking adoption has increased, but the branch network remains expensive to maintain. Overall bank profit is stable, but branch-level profitability is declining.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the profitability of the branch network and recommend a path forward?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue and cost by branch
Reviews customer traffic, deposits, and product sales
Examines staffing, rent, and operating costs
Considers digital adoption and customer behavior
great_answer_includes: Segments branches by profitability and strategic importance
Calculates cost-to-serve by customer segment
Evaluates consolidation, format changes, or advisory-focused branches
Balances cost savings against customer retention risk
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should avoid recommending blanket closures and instead segment branches by economics and strategic value.

title: Industrial Pump Manufacturer Margin Decline
background: An industrial pump manufacturer sells to energy, mining, and municipal customers. Revenue has been stable, but profit margins have declined due to rising production costs and inconsistent demand across customer segments.
candidate_prompt: How would you diagnose the margin decline and identify improvement opportunities?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue and margin by customer segment
Reviews raw material, labor, and overhead costs
Examines product mix and pricing
Considers production efficiency and capacity utilization
great_answer_includes: Identifies low-margin custom products or customer segments
Quantifies impact of raw material cost changes
Evaluates pricing pass-through ability by contract type
Recommends product standardization, pricing changes, or customer prioritization
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should connect margin decline to product mix, input costs, pricing power, and manufacturing utilization.

title: Private Tutoring Company Profit Decline
background: A private tutoring company serves middle school and high school students through online and in-person sessions. Revenue has increased, but profits are down because the company has hired more tutors and expanded marketing.
candidate_prompt: How would you determine whether the company’s growth is profitable and what changes should be made?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue by student type and service format
Reviews tutor wages and utilization
Examines marketing costs and customer acquisition
Compares online versus in-person margins
great_answer_includes: Calculates tutor utilization and contribution margin per session
Segments customers by acquisition channel and retention
Evaluates pricing differences across subjects or formats
Recommends scheduling, pricing, and marketing efficiency improvements
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should focus on unit economics by session, tutor utilization, and customer acquisition payback.

title: Residential Solar Installer Profitability Issue
background: A residential solar installation company has grown quickly across three states. Installations are up 40%, but profit margins have declined due to longer project timelines, higher labor costs, and more customer cancellations.
candidate_prompt: How would you analyze the company’s profitability problem and recommend improvements?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue and margin per installation
Reviews labor, equipment, permitting, and sales costs
Examines cancellation rates and project delays
Compares profitability across states or sales channels
great_answer_includes: Calculates contribution margin by installation type
Identifies bottlenecks in permitting or crew scheduling
Evaluates sales commission structure and cancellation risk
Recommends operational improvements and tighter customer qualification
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should recognize that installation volume growth can hurt margins if execution capacity and sales quality are weak.

title: Movie Theater Chain Profit Recovery
background: A movie theater chain has seen ticket sales recover after a difficult period, but profits remain below expectations. Management wants to understand whether the issue is ticket pricing, concessions, labor, or changing customer habits.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the theater chain’s profitability and identify the best improvement levers?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes admissions revenue and ticket pricing
Reviews concessions revenue and margins
Examines labor, rent, and utility costs
Considers attendance patterns and movie release schedules
great_answer_includes: Calculates profit contribution per guest
Identifies concession attachment rate as a key driver
Evaluates premium seating or subscription models
Recommends pricing, bundling, and staffing improvements
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should look beyond ticket volume and evaluate per-guest economics.

title: Beauty Salon Group Profit Decline
background: A beauty salon group operates 25 locations offering haircuts, coloring, skincare, and retail products. Revenue is stable, but profit has declined over the past year. The owner wants to understand which services or costs are causing the issue.
candidate_prompt: How would you analyze the profit decline and recommend actions?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue by service and product category
Reviews stylist utilization and labor costs
Examines pricing and appointment volume
Considers rent, supplies, and retail product margins
great_answer_includes: Calculates margin by service type and stylist hour
Identifies underutilized time slots or low-margin services
Evaluates upsell opportunities in retail products
Recommends pricing, scheduling, and service mix changes
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should focus on labor utilization and service-level profitability.

title: Specialty Produce Farm Margin Pressure
background: A specialty produce farm sells organic berries and leafy greens to grocery chains, restaurants, and farmers markets. Revenue has grown, but profits have declined due to spoilage, labor costs, and inconsistent wholesale pricing.
candidate_prompt: How would you determine what is causing the profitability decline and what should the farm do?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue and margin by crop and sales channel
Reviews labor, packaging, transportation, and spoilage costs
Examines pricing differences across customers
Considers seasonality and yield variability
great_answer_includes: Quantifies spoilage impact by crop and channel
Identifies whether wholesale growth is diluting margins
Evaluates harvest planning and demand forecasting
Recommends channel mix, pricing, and waste reduction initiatives
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should consider perishable inventory dynamics and channel profitability.

title: Cybersecurity Consulting Firm Margin Decline
background: A cybersecurity consulting firm serves mid-market companies with risk assessments, implementation projects, and managed monitoring services. Revenue has increased, but profit margins have declined as the firm has hired aggressively.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the firm’s profitability and recommend improvements?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue and margin by service line
Reviews consultant utilization and billing rates
Examines hiring, training, and overhead costs
Considers project pricing and scope creep
great_answer_includes: Calculates utilization and margin by consultant level
Identifies low-margin custom projects or underpriced work
Evaluates recurring managed services versus project work
Recommends pricing discipline, staffing model changes, and scope controls
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should understand the importance of utilization, billing rates, and project scope in professional services profitability.
